full_response:
Below “d” denotes spacetime dimension; here d=4.

We are given 
  R_{abcd} = −(1/l²)\,(g_{ac} g_{bd} − g_{ad} g_{bc}) . 

The Ricci tensor is by definition  
  R_{bd} ≡ g^{ac} R_{abcd} . 

Step 1:  Contract g^{ac} into Riemann:
  R_{bd}
  = g^{ac}\big[−(1/l²)(g_{ac} g_{bd} − g_{ad} g_{bc})\big] 
  = −(1/l²)\,\big[g^{ac}g_{ac}\;g_{bd} − g^{ac}g_{ad}\;g_{bc}\big] .

Step 2:  Use g^{ac} g_{ac}=δ^a_a=d=4,  and g^{ac}g_{ad}=δ^c_d so that δ^c_d g_{bc}=g_{bd}.  Thus
  R_{bd}
  = −(1/l²)\,\big[4 g_{bd} − g_{bd}\big]
  = −(3/l²)\,g_{bd} . 

Hence in this spacetime 
  R_{μν} = −(3/l²)\,g_{μν} . 

Boxing the final result:

\boxed{R_{\mu\nu} = -\frac{3}{l^2}\,g_{\mu\nu}}

Confidence: 100%
